summary: Disambiguate the three T-SQL surfaces in Fabric — SQL analytics endpoint (read-only, auto-provisioned over a Lakehouse), Warehouse (read-write analytics), and SQL Database (OLTP). They look similar but are distinct items with different capabilities.

meanings.description: Read-only T-SQL surface auto-provisioned over a Lakehouse's Delta tables. You cannot create/deploy it independently (Fabric manages it). Use it to query lakehouse tables with T-SQL.
example_commands: fabio sql-endpoint query --workspace $WS --id $EP --sql "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dbo.orders"

meanings.description: Full read-write T-SQL analytics warehouse (DDL/DML, statistics, query insights). Use for a managed analytics warehouse you own end-to-end.
Transactional (OLTP) SQL database in Fabric. Requires F4+ capacity (F2 fails with error 18456 State 240). Use for app/transactional workloads, not analytics.

default_action: Analytics + read-only over a lakehouse -> sql-endpoint. Analytics + read-write warehouse -> warehouse. Transactional/app database -> sql-database.
